Risk Description: Because of delays in completing Pre-Development deliverables (User Stories, UX Design, Functional Requirements, HLD approvals) across multiple releases (0.2-0.8), there is a risk that development cycles will be compressed and downstream activities (UAT, Release 1 go-live) will be delayed, which could lead to cascading schedule impacts, potential SLA breach, and increased project costs due to extended timelines.
Potential Impact: Cascading delays to UAT and Release 1 go-live; potential SLA breach; increased project costs due to extended timelines; compressed development and testing cycles.

Task/Item: AWS Bedrock Setup and Pipeline Integration
Risk Description: Because of pending AWS Bedrock setup, ADO to AWS pipeline integration, and FMS configuration for Billie Part 2 (AI invoice processing using Claude Sonnet 4.5), there is a risk that Release 0.7.1 development and testing will be delayed by 4+ weeks after infrastructure readiness, which could lead to delayed UAT start, Release 1 go-live at risk, and AI-driven invoice processing unavailable for beta customers.
Tech Stack: AWS Bedrock, Claude Sonnet 4.5, ADO Pipeline, FMS
Potential Impact: Delayed UAT start; Release 1 go-live at risk; AI-driven invoice processing unavailable for beta customers; potential contract penalties; reputational damage.

Task/Item: Power BI Infrastructure and Reporting Requirements
SLA Metric: Data gateway and workspace setup pending
Risk Description: Because of incomplete Power BI infrastructure setup (data gateway, workspace configuration) and pending functional requirements for standard reports and reporting center, there is a risk that Release 0.8 (Reporting) development will be delayed, which could lead to reporting functionality unavailable for Release 1, business intelligence gaps, and delayed decision-making capabilities.
Tech Stack: Power BI, Data Gateway, SQL Server 2019
Potential Impact: Reporting functionality unavailable for Release 1; business intelligence gaps; delayed decision-making capabilities; potential compliance reporting issues.

Task/Item: Functional Requirements and HLD Approvals - Payments/Disbursements
Risk Description: Because of pending approvals for Functional Requirements (7/31), Design (8/8), Data Models (8/1), and HLD for Payments/Disbursements module, there is a risk that development start will be delayed beyond the planned 7/15 date, which could lead to development rework, compressed testing timelines, potential quality issues, and Release 1 timeline at risk.
Potential Impact: Development rework; compressed testing timelines; potential quality issues; Release 1 timeline at risk; increased defect density.

Task/Item: User Research and Requirements - Vendors Module
Risk Description: Because of incomplete user research and pending approvals for Functional Requirements (8/1), Design (8/1), Data Models, and HLD for the Vendors module, there is a risk that the revised development start date of 8/4 will result in a compressed development cycle, which could lead to increased defect risk, potential functionality gaps, and UAT quality concerns.
Potential Impact: Compressed development cycle; increased defect risk; potential functionality gaps; UAT quality concerns; Release 1 scope reduction risk.

Risk Description: Because of pending HLD approvals for Approval Engine (Kannan, ETA 8/1) and Approval UI HLD (Keith, ETA 7/30), and development already started with HLD 7.2, there is a risk of significant architectural rework and misalignment, which could lead to integration challenges, increased defect density, and potential security and compliance gaps in approval workflows.
Potential Impact: Architectural rework; integration challenges; increased defect density; potential security and compliance gaps in approval workflows; schedule delays.

Risk Description: Because of 116 QA defects identified across sprints 15-20 and a defect leakage rate of approximately 4.8% (approaching the 5% threshold), there is a risk that escaped defects will reach UAT and production environments, which could lead to customer-reported defects, UAT delays, production issues, potential SLA breach, increased support costs, and reputational risk.
Potential Impact: Customer-reported defects; UAT delays; production issues; potential SLA breach; increased support costs; reputational risk; customer dissatisfaction.

Risk Description: Because UAT for Release 0.4+0.8 was paused and a complete UAT readiness assessment from 0.1 through 0.4 is required before re-release to Axos, there is a risk that accumulated technical debt, integration issues, and regression defects will surface during comprehensive UAT, which could lead to extended UAT cycles, delayed go-live, increased defect discovery in UAT, potential scope reduction, and customer dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Extended UAT cycles; delayed go-live; increased defect discovery in UAT; potential scope reduction; customer dissatisfaction; contract penalties.

Risk Description: Because of recurring environment downtimes totaling 181 hours of team productivity loss since May 14, 2026, there is a risk that development, testing, and integration activities will continue to be disrupted, which could lead to continued productivity loss, schedule delays, reduced code quality, team morale issues, and increased project costs.
Potential Impact: Continued productivity loss; schedule delays; reduced code quality; team morale issues; increased project costs; potential SLA breach.

Risk Description: Because HLD completion and approval for Release 0.2 modules was delayed until July 29-31, and development was already complete, there is a risk that architectural misalignments will require rework and impact future releases, which could lead to architectural rework, integration challenges, potential defects, schedule impacts for future releases, and increased technical debt.
Potential Impact: Architectural rework; integration challenges; potential defects; schedule impacts for future releases; increased technical debt; quality degradation.

Risk Description: Because of the planned UI revamp using Play+ framework requiring installation in Axos environment (Phase 1: 7/23-8/7) and updating existing screens (Phase 2: 8/11-9/8), there is a risk that implementation challenges, compatibility issues, or scope expansion will cause delays or quality issues, which could lead to UI inconsistencies, integration issues, UAT delays, user adoption challenges, and potential rework of existing screens.
Potential Impact: UI inconsistencies; integration issues; UAT delays; user adoption challenges; potential rework of existing screens; user experience degradation.

Financial Metric: Fixed price: Schedule B; Hourly rate: $60-120/hr
Risk Description: Because Phase 2 operates under a fixed-price model with change management requiring either fixed-price quotes, hourly rates ($60-120/hr), or use of 300 Accrued Hours (25 hrs/month over 12 months), there is a risk that scope changes, rework, or unplanned activities will consume the accrued hours buffer and require additional budget approvals, which could lead to budget overruns, contract disputes, scope compression, potential descoping of features, and stakeholder dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Budget overruns; contract disputes; scope compression; potential descoping of features; stakeholder dissatisfaction; delayed approvals.

Risk Description: Because Phase 2 scope includes integration with third-party payroll provider Rollfi (19 UI/UX screens, 59 APIs) and functional parity with Current Zenith System is required, there is a risk that integration complexity, API changes, or functional gaps will cause delays or quality issues, which could lead to functional parity gaps, integration delays, scope reduction, customer dissatisfaction, and potential need for alternative providers.
Potential Impact: Functional parity gaps; integration delays; scope reduction; customer dissatisfaction; potential need for alternative providers; contract penalties.

Risk Description: Because Axos Bank will primarily handle integration of banking products and services with Rebuilt Zenith System, and Ascendion must verify functional parity with Current Zenith System, there is a risk that late-stage functional parity gaps will be discovered during integration testing, which could lead to late-stage integration gaps, rework, schedule delays, consumption of Accrued Hours, and potential functional parity issues.
Tech Stack: Banking APIs, Core Banking System, Integration Layer
Potential Impact: Late-stage integration gaps; rework; schedule delays; consumption of Accrued Hours; potential functional parity issues; customer impact.

SLA Metric: Multiple features deferred from R1 to R2
Risk Description: Because multiple features have been deferred from Release 1 to Release 2 (including International Wires, ACH Confirmations, Credit Card Auto-Allocation, Dashboard Widgets, Task Management enhancements), there is a risk that Release 1 will deliver reduced business value and impact customer onboarding plans, which could lead to reduced Release 1 business value, delayed customer onboarding, potential customer dissatisfaction, and competitive disadvantage.
Potential Impact: Reduced Release 1 business value; delayed customer onboarding; potential customer dissatisfaction; competitive disadvantage; contract renegotiation.

Risk Description: Because unit test coverage improved from 60% to 70% with AAVA generating 90% of unit test cases, but 30% of code remains untested, there is a risk that untested code paths will contain defects that escape to later testing stages and production, which could lead to production defects in untested code, customer impact, increased support costs, and potential SLA breaches.
Potential Impact: Production defects in untested code; customer impact; increased support costs; potential SLA breaches; technical debt accumulation.

Risk Description: Because performance requirements are stringent (UI page rendering <=10 seconds, API response <=3 seconds) and performance testing strategy discussions are scheduled for 3/3, there is a risk that performance testing will identify bottlenecks requiring significant code refactoring or infrastructure upgrades, which could lead to performance SLA breaches, code refactoring required, hardware capacity upgrades, delayed go-live, and customer dissatisfaction.
Tech Stack: Performance Testing Tools, Load Testing, Angular 18, .NET 8
Potential Impact: Performance SLA breaches; code refactoring required; hardware capacity upgrades; delayed go-live; customer dissatisfaction; contract penalties.

Risk Description: Because data migration strategy and estimates are not yet finalized, and the ability to have 5 beta customers ready at go-live depends on successful data migration from Legacy Zenith to Rebuilt Zenith, there is a risk that data migration complexity, data quality issues, or timeline constraints will delay customer onboarding, which could lead to delayed customer onboarding, go-live delays, data quality issues, potential manual data entry, and increased project costs.
Tech Stack: Data Migration Tools, SQL Server 2019, ETL Processes
Potential Impact: Delayed customer onboarding; go-live delays; data quality issues; potential manual data entry; increased project costs; customer dissatisfaction.

SLA Metric: DNS changes implemented; UI issues pending
Risk Description: Because AAVA 2.0 installation in Axos environment required DNS changes (completed) but has open UI issues (Execute tab, Agent Edit screen, KB creation visibility), and AKS cluster setup and agent deployment are pending, there is a risk that AAVA 2.0 adoption will be delayed or teams will revert to AAVA 1.0, which could lead to reduced productivity gains, inability to leverage AI capabilities, potential reversion to AAVA 1.0, and schedule impacts.
Tech Stack: AAVA 2.0, AKS Cluster, AI Agents, Angular 18
Potential Impact: Reduced productivity gains; inability to leverage AI capabilities; potential reversion to AAVA 1.0; schedule impacts; lost innovation opportunity.

Risk Description: Because regression automation coverage is at 80% across sprints 15-20, leaving 20% of regression testing manual, there is a risk that manual regression testing will be time-consuming, error-prone, and delay release cycles, which could lead to manual regression testing delays, defect leakage, UAT delays, increased QA costs, and potential production issues.
Tech Stack: QA Automation Tools, Test Management, JIRA
Potential Impact: Manual regression testing delays; defect leakage; UAT delays; increased QA costs; potential production issues; schedule compression.

Risk Description: Because public and private API categorization is in progress and requires comprehensive documentation of background processes and API endpoints (ETA 3/9), and all private APIs must be protected with proper authorization controls, there is a risk that API security gaps or authorization vulnerabilities will be discovered late in the cycle, which could lead to security vulnerabilities, authorization gaps, compliance violations, potential security breaches, and reputational damage.
Potential Impact: Security vulnerabilities; authorization gaps; compliance violations; potential security breaches; reputational damage; audit failures.

Risk Description: Because BurpSuite security scan results have been extracted but not yet acted upon, there is a risk that identified security vulnerabilities will remain unresolved and could be exploited, which could lead to unresolved security vulnerabilities, compliance violations, security breaches, delayed production deployment, and audit failures.
Tech Stack: BurpSuite, Security Testing Tools, Vulnerability Management
Potential Impact: Unresolved security vulnerabilities; compliance violations; security breaches; delayed production deployment; audit failures; regulatory fines.

Risk Description: Because database models for Deposits/Receipts/Commissions went through multiple changes resulting in rework, and shared services component dependencies exist, there is a risk that continued data model instability will cause ongoing rework and integration issues, which could lead to continued rework, integration issues, schedule delays, defects, quality concerns, and increased technical debt.
Tech Stack: SQL Server 2019, Data Modeling Tools, Entity Framework
Potential Impact: Continued rework; integration issues; schedule delays; defects; quality concerns; increased technical debt; data integrity issues.

SLA Metric: ~22.19 bugs per KLOC; Target: 2 bugs per KLOC
Risk Description: Because actual defect density is approximately 22.19 bugs per KLOC (thousand lines of code) compared to the target of 2 bugs per KLOC, exceeding the target by over 10x, there is a risk that systemic code quality issues exist and will result in high defect rates in production, which could lead to systemic code quality issues, increased maintenance costs, production defects, customer dissatisfaction, and technical debt accumulation.
Potential Impact: Systemic code quality issues; increased maintenance costs; production defects; customer dissatisfaction; technical debt accumulation; SLA breaches.

Risk Description: Because cross-POD dependencies were not fully identified upfront, leading to coordination challenges across Alpha, Cypher, Nexus, and Apex teams, there is a risk that integration issues, conflicting changes, and dependency delays will impact delivery quality and schedule, which could lead to integration issues, conflicting changes, dependency delays, schedule impacts, quality issues, and team coordination challenges.
Tech Stack: Project Management Tools, JIRA, Collaboration Tools
Potential Impact: Integration issues; conflicting changes; dependency delays; schedule impacts; quality issues; team coordination challenges; rework.

SLA Metric: Historical loss of key resources noted
Risk Description: Because historical project data shows loss of key team members (DB Architects, Solution Architect, Product Owner, Delivery Leader, Testing Lead) over 12 months, there is a risk that continued attrition will result in knowledge loss and productivity decline, which could lead to knowledge loss, productivity decline, schedule delays, increased onboarding costs, and quality impacts.
Potential Impact: Knowledge loss; productivity decline; schedule delays; increased onboarding costs; quality impacts; team morale issues.

Risk Description: Because token-based authentication has an accepted vulnerability where tokens remain valid until short expiration time even after logout, and this is an accepted risk across multiple projects, there is a risk that unauthorized access could occur within the token validity window, which could lead to potential unauthorized access, security incidents, compliance concerns, and limited window of vulnerability.
Potential Impact: Potential unauthorized access; security incidents; compliance concerns; limited window of vulnerability; audit findings.

Risk Description: Because Axos follows an N-1 versioning strategy for all software (currently Angular 18, .NET 8, SQL Server 2019, HTTP 1.1) and upgrades only when necessary, there is a risk that delayed upgrades will accumulate technical debt and create security or compatibility issues, which could lead to technical debt accumulation, security vulnerabilities, compatibility issues, increased maintenance costs, and potential upgrade disruption.
Potential Impact: Technical debt accumulation; security vulnerabilities; compatibility issues; increased maintenance costs; potential upgrade disruption.

SLA Metric: Zero critical/high defects in R0.2 regression
Risk Description: Because Release 0.2 achieved zero critical or high defects in Ascendion regression testing, setting a high-quality bar, there is a risk that subsequent releases may not maintain this quality level due to schedule pressure or complexity, which could lead to quality degradation in future releases, defect leakage, UAT issues, potential production defects, and customer dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Quality degradation in future releases; defect leakage; UAT issues; potential production defects; customer dissatisfaction; reputational damage.

Risk Description: Because standard reports requirements and reporting center requirements are pending Axos review and feedback on balance sheet (functional requirements and UX/UI update ETA 8/22), there is a risk that delayed requirements finalization will compress development cycles for Release 0.8 (Reporting), which could lead to compressed development cycles, incomplete reporting functionality, defective reports, Release 1 gaps, and business intelligence limitations.
Tech Stack: Power BI, Reporting Framework, SQL Server 2019
Potential Impact: Compressed development cycles; incomplete reporting functionality; defective reports; Release 1 gaps; business intelligence limitations; decision-making delays.
